markdown:
#  Edit a User Account in the Company Directory 
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/permissions)
  * Updating a user profile in the Company Directory automatically syncs the changes across all their assigned projects.
  * While you cannot delete user accounts from the Company Directory, you can deactivate them. See [Deactivate User Accounts in the Company Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/inactivate-a-batch-of-user-accounts-in-the-company-directory).
  * Updating this email address may not change the user's login email address. See [Can I change a person's email address in the Directory?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-can-i-change-a-persons-email-address-in-the-directory)
  * If you have the Resource Planning tool and data syncs enabled, shared fields in the Personal Information section are updated in both tools. See [What People Information Syncs between the Procore Company Directory and Resource Planning?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-people-information-is-synced-between-resource-planning-and-the-procore-company-directory)
  * Editing a user's login:
    * Company Directory admins on an SSO domain can update login emails for coworkers in that same domain, or other users who only have user accounts in your company's Procore account. See [Can I change a person's email address in the Directory?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-can-i-change-a-persons-email-address-in-the-directory)
    * Without SSO, users can change their login email in ‘My Profile Settings’ if the new address isn't already used in Procore. See [Change Your Login ID Email Address for Your Procore User Account](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/portfolio-company/tutorials/change-your-login-id-email-address-for-your-procore-user-account).


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the Company level **Directory** tool.
  2. Locate the user record to modify. Then click **Edit**.
  3. _Optional:_ If the user is employed by or affiliated with a company already in the Company level Directory, you can update the user's personal information with the information from the company by selecting the company in the 'Company Name' drop-down menu and clicking **Copy to Personal Information**.
  4. Update the user's Personal Information. **Show/Hide Fields**
     * **First Name**. The user's first name.
     * **Last Name**. The users' last name.
     * **Initials**. The user's initials.
     * **Email Address (Admin Only)**. The email address where the user receives Procore notifications.  
_Notes:_
       * If the user has access to more than one company's Procore account, you may not be able to change that user's email address.
       * Updating this email address may not change the user's login email address. See [Can I change a person's email address in the Directory?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-can-i-change-a-persons-email-address-in-the-directory)
     * **Login**. Edit the user's login information. This is the specific email address that receives security-related communications, such as password resets. 
     * **Address**. The user's address.
     * **City**. The city associated with the user's address.
     * **Country/State**. The state associated with the user's address.
     * **ZIP**. The ZIP code associated with the user's address.
     * **Business Phone**. The user's business phone number.
     * **Business Fax**. The user's fax number.
     * **Cell Phone**. The user's mobile phone number.
     * **Job Title**. The user's job title.
     * **Tags/Keywords**. Any tags and keywords for the user.
     * _Admin Only:_ The following fields can only be viewed and updated by users with 'Admin' level permissions on the Company level Directory tool.
       * **Active?** A checkmark indicates that the user is active.
       * **Classification**. A classification for the user. See [Add a Classification](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/add-a-classification).
       * **Send This User Messages**. Users can receive messages from Procore 'Via Email' or 'Not at All'.  
_Notes:_
         * Setting this to ‘Not at All’ stops all Procore emails, including password resets, invitations, and distribution list messages.
         * If you will be designating this user as an [invoice contact](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-an-invoice-contact), always select 'Via Email' to ensure that any invoice-related messages are sent. 
       * **Is Employee Of [Company Name]?**. A checkmark indicates that the user is an employee of your company. 
       * **Is an Insurance Manager of [Company Name]?** A checkmark indicates that the user is an Insurance Manager for your company.
       * **Attachments**. 
         1. Click **Attach File(s)** or use a drag-and-drop to select a file on your computer and attach it to the user's record.
  5. Save the information:
     * _Optional:_ Mark the checkbox to send an invitation.
     * Click **Save** or **Save and Manage Projects**.


#####  Tip
To edit Resource Planning fields from the Directory, see [Edit Resource Planning Information for People](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/resource-planning-company/tutorials/edit-people-information-for-resource-planning).
## See Also
  * [Deactivate User Accounts in the Company Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/inactivate-a-batch-of-user-accounts-in-the-company-directory)
  * [Unlock a User Account in the Company Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/unlock-a-user-account-in-the-company-directory)
  * [Add Someone as an Employee of Your Company](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/add-someone-as-an-employee-of-your-company)


In Procore, the term _Insurance Manager_ refers to an internal employee (or multiple employees) at your company who serve as your organization's primary point of contact(s) for ensuring that the insurance policies for your contractors, subcontractors, and other vendors are in compliance with requirements and that their required policies and certificates are kept up-to-date.
title: Edit a User Account in the Company Directory
description: How to edit a user account in the Company level Directory tool.
